description_long: This object is a delicate yellow wildflower with multiple slender green stems branching out from a central base. The main stem supports a prominent, open flower with five broad, rounded petals arranged in a radial pattern, each petal displaying a soft, pale yellow hue. Surrounding the main bloom are several smaller buds and partially opened flowers, giving the plant a natural, clustered appearance. The stems are thin and flexible, with a few deeply lobed, green leaves attached near the lower portion. The overall structure is airy and lightweight, typical of wildflowers found in urban parks, roadside verges, or garden beds. The flower's surface appears soft and matte, with a gentle, natural sheen on the petals.
description: A pale yellow wildflower with five rounded petals, slender green stems, and several small buds. The plant features lobed green leaves and a natural, clustered arrangement.
description_view_0: Side view showing the main open flower and several buds, with the stems and leaves visible from the left.
description_view_1: Frontal view highlighting the open flower facing forward, with buds and leaves arranged behind and below.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the back of the main flower and the cluster of buds, with stems and leaves visible from the right.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the main flower and the arrangement of buds above, with the stems and leaves mostly behind.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, actinomorphic pattern with five distinct, overlapping petals forming a symmetrical whorl around the flower's center.
